# W7T2 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal
Add safe, read-only storage access on top of `RawStorage`. This task must expose bounded element access and full backing-range slice access, while preserving the storage and tensor responsibility split.

## Related Design Documents
- `build/SUMMARY.md`, Wave 7, `W7T2`
- `design/05-storage.md` §5.5, §5.5a, §9.0, §9.1
- `design/00-coding.md` §5.1, §6.3

## Dependencies
- `W7T1`

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/storage/mod.rs`

## Implementation Details
Add the exact trait definition:

```rust
pub unsafe trait Storage: RawStorage {
    #[inline]
    fn get(&self, index: usize) -> Option<&Self::Elem> {
        if index < self.len() {
            Some(unsafe { &*self.as_ptr().add(index) })
        } else {
            None
        }
    }

    #[inline]
    unsafe fn get_unchecked(&self, index: usize) -> &Self::Elem {
        &*self.as_ptr().add(index)
    }

    #[inline]
    fn as_slice(&self) -> &[Self::Elem] {
        unsafe { core::slice::from_raw_parts(self.as_ptr(), self.len()) }
    }
}
```

Important semantic constraints:
- `as_slice()` spans exactly `len()` initialized elements from the storage base pointer
- do not expose hidden capacity or padding
- do not apply tensor-level `offset`, `shape`, or `strides`

## Test Plan
- `test_storage_compile`
- `test_storage_get_in_bounds`
- `test_storage_get_out_of_bounds`
- `test_storage_as_slice_matches_backing_range`

Test scenarios:
- `get(0)` returns the first element
- `get(len)` returns `None`
- `as_slice()` length equals `len()`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `Storage` extends `RawStorage` exactly.
- `get`, `get_unchecked`, and `as_slice` signatures match the design.
- Safety comments explain the bounds-checking preconditions.
- Documentation states clearly that `as_slice` is storage-level, not tensor-level.

## Development Workflow
1. Write failing tests for in-bounds and out-of-bounds reads.
2. Implement `Storage` with the exact default methods.
3. Run unit tests on a mock storage type.
4. Re-read the docs to confirm no tensor-level semantics leaked into the trait.
